Note: verifier accepts any of 9 antihypertensive classes (amlodipine, lisinopril, losartan, metoprolol, atenolol, hydrochlorothiazide, valsartan, ramipril, enalapril) per export_result.sh lines 27-29

pi_items.verified_value: I10 is the ICD-10-CM code for Essential (primary) hypertension, a diagnosis of high blood pressure without identifiable secondary cause
pi_items.source: ICD-10-CM 2026 official codes database (icd10data.com). setup_task.sh line 50 confirms diagnosis_name='Essential (primary) hypertension'

pi_items.metadata_value: amlodipine is expected as a first-line antihypertensive
pi_items.verified_value: Amlodipine is recognized as a first-line antihypertensive agent with strong evidence for cardiovascular event reduction
pi_items.source: AAFP guidelines and peer-reviewed literature on antihypertensive therapy (PMC5051471 and NCBI StatPearls). Lisinopril (ACE inhibitor) is also first-line per AAFP.

privileged_info_summary: Task hypertension_panel_remediation@1 involves 5 synthetic patients seeded in NOSH ChartingSystem: three untreated hypertensive patients (pids 22-24: Eleanor Whitfield DOB 1962-03-15, Russell Hartley DOB 1955-08-22, Margaret Toomey DOB 1958-11-07, all with I10 diagnosis and no active antihypertensive medications) and two already-treated patients (pids 25-26: Bernard Keane DOB 1960-04-30 on Lisinopril 10mg, Dolores Vance DOB 1963-09-12 on Amlodipine 5mg, included as noise). The expected medication is amlodipine (though verifier accepts any of 9 first-line antihypertensive classes). Pass threshold is 60/100 points, requiring either 3 antihypertensive prescriptions or 2 prescriptions plus 2+ encounter notes. Data is entirely synthetic and seeded by setup_task.sh.
